pain_point_label: New Campus Launch Readiness & Curriculum Deployment

matches.match_rationale: New campus launches require generating a large volume of standardized documents — onboarding guides, curriculum syllabi summaries, faculty orientation materials, and launch communications — rapidly and consistently to network standards. Without AI, this assembly is ad-hoc and time-consuming, leading to incomplete preparation for first cohorts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted launch content generator that produces campus-specific versions of onboarding packets, welcome communications, and curriculum guides from network templates in hours rather than weeks.

matches.match_rationale: Curriculum materials exist across multiple sources (shared drives, legacy campuses, vendor portals) and newly hired faculty cannot quickly locate or assemble the right resources to network standards. A RAG system grounded in the operator's curriculum corpus lets faculty ask natural-language questions and receive authoritative, cited answers about what materials to use and how.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A curriculum knowledge base assistant where faculty at a new campus can query 'What do I need for Week 3 of the foundational literacy module?' and receive structured, source-linked answers drawn from the network's existing materials.

matches.match_rationale: A campus launch involves dozens of interdependent steps across HR, curriculum, facilities, and IT — each with dependencies that, when unresolved, cascade into launch delays and under-prepared first cohorts. An orchestration agent can track task completion, surface unresolved dependencies, trigger stakeholder reminders, and flag at-risk milestones automatically, replacing the ad-hoc checklist problem.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A launch readiness automation agent that ingests a go-live checklist template, tracks completion status across systems, sends automated nudges to responsible parties, and produces a real-time readiness dashboard for network leadership.

matches.match_rationale: Assembling a coherent curriculum package for a new campus requires satisfying multiple simultaneous constraints: regulatory requirements, faculty competency coverage, available materials, sequencing dependencies, and cohort start dates. AI constraint solving can automatically identify a valid curriculum configuration from the network's library that meets all hard requirements for a given campus context.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A curriculum configuration tool that, given a campus profile (grade levels, student population, regulatory jurisdiction, available faculty), automatically assembles a compliant, complete curriculum package from the network's resource library.

matches.match_rationale: Campus launch readiness spans multiple domains simultaneously — curriculum assembly, faculty onboarding, compliance verification, and logistics coordination — each requiring different expertise and data sources. A multi-agent system with specialized sub-agents for each domain, coordinated by a launch orchestrator, can parallelize and cross-check readiness across all tracks simultaneously.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A launch readiness multi-agent pipeline where a curriculum agent, an HR onboarding agent, and a compliance agent each run independently and surface blockers to a coordinating launch manager agent that produces a unified go/no-go readiness report.
